channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jeremy Corbyn’s supporters secure majority on Your Party executive, paving way for parliamentary leadership
item.description: Jeremy Corbyn is expected to become the parliamentary leader of Your Party after his supporters secured a majority on the party’s central executive committee (CEC). The faction backing the former Labo...

item.title: Arsenal favoured to win Champions League, while Tottenham's chances are slim after knockout stage draw
item.description: Arsenal are the favourites to win their first-ever Champions League title, according to the Opta supercomputer, with a 27.4% chance of lifting the trophy. The Premier League leaders, who are also vyin...

item.title: Two British men arrested in Benidorm over alleged fake kidnapping plot to obtain ransom
item.description: Two British men aged 37 and 51 have been arrested in Benidorm, Spain, on suspicion of staging a fake kidnapping in an attempt to extort €830 (£728) from a relative in the United Kingdom. According to ...

item.title: Pakistan launches airstrikes on Kabul and other Afghan provinces, declares 'open war' on Taliban government amid escalating border conflict
item.description: Tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan's Taliban administration have dramatically escalated, with Pakistan conducting airstrikes on targets in Kabul, Kandahar, and Paktia provinces in the early hou...

item.title: Drug dealer sentenced to 16 years after fatal chase in Sittingbourne
item.description: A 43-year-old man has been sentenced to 16 years in prison after admitting the manslaughter of Jay Gerrett, who died following a confrontation linked to a drug deal in Sittingbourne, Kent. Chris Macle...

item.title: Rare Victorian Photograph Inspiring Led Zeppelin IV Cover Sells for £16,000 at Auction
item.description: A rare 19th-century Victorian photograph of Wiltshire thatcher Lot Long has fetched £16,000 at an auction held by Denhams in Horsham, West Sussex. This large-format print, measuring 60.8 x 44.4 cm, is...

item.title: Residents of Jerusalem React to Rising Tensions Amid Potential Middle East Conflict
item.description: In the Old City of Jerusalem, residents are feeling the weight of growing tensions in the Middle East, with fears of a potential conflict involving the US and Iran. Shopkeepers report a sharp decline ...

item.title: Metropolitan Police Apologises to Commons Speaker for Revealing Tip-Off Source in Lord Mandelson Arrest
item.description: The Metropolitan Police has issued an apology to the Commons Speaker, Sir Lindsay Hoyle, for inadvertently disclosing during an ongoing investigation that he was the source of a tip-off concerning Lor...

item.title: Green Party secures historic win in Gorton and Denton by-election as Labour falls to third
item.description: The Green Party achieved a historic victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election, marking its first parliamentary seat in northern England. Hannah Spencer, a local plumber and Green councillor, overtu...

item.title: Collagen Supplements Can Improve Skin Health, But Not a Quick Anti-Wrinkle Fix, Study Finds
item.description: A new review from Anglia Ruskin University analyzed 16 reviews and 113 trials with nearly 8,000 participants to investigate the effects of collagen supplements on skin health. The research concluded t...

item.title: Man Jailed for Leaving Partner Paralysed After Brutal Attack When She Tried to Leave
item.description: Robert Easom, 57, has been sentenced to 16 years in prison after a brutal assault left his former partner, Trudi Burgess, paralysed. The assault followed a campaign of coercive and controlling behavio...

item.title: BBC investigation links Nepal police chief to lethal force order during September Gen Z protests
item.description: A BBC World Service investigation has revealed that an order authorizing the use of lethal force against largely young and unarmed protesters during demonstrations in Kathmandu on 8 September last yea...

item.title: Ellen Jantzen’s ‘Disturbing the Spirits’ Explores the Ethereal Beauty of Winter Trees
item.description: The article highlights the haunting and meditative photography of St. Louis-based artist Ellen Jantzen, focusing on her series “Disturbing the Spirits.” Rather than simply documenting landscapes, Jant...

item.title: Labour calls for investigation into reported ‘family voting’ in Gorton and Denton by-election
item.description: Labour has called on the relevant authorities to investigate allegations of widespread ‘family voting’ during the recent Gorton and Denton by-election. The party’s chairwoman described the reports as ...

item.title: Docudramas, Media Intrusion and the Limits of Public Scrutiny
item.description: Simon Jenkins examines the growing tension between media dramatization and factual reporting, particularly in the context of high-profile public figures. Recent docudramas, including 'The Lady' and 'D...

item.title: IronCurtain Introduces Policy-Based Controls to Secure AI Agents in Isolated Environments
item.description: A new open source project called IronCurtain aims to address growing security concerns surrounding autonomous AI agents that manage users’ digital lives. As agentic assistants gain popularity for hand...

item.title: James Webb Space Telescope Discovers Early Barred Spiral Galaxy
item.description: Astronomers have identified a surprising discovery in the early universe, with the James Webb Space Telescope revealing a barred spiral galaxy known as COSMOS-74706. This galaxy, located roughly 11.5 ...

item.title: Rotary Club of Jinja launches 4th Run for the Nile to raise sh550 million for environmental conservation
item.description: The Rotary Club of Jinja has officially launched the fourth edition of the Run for the Nile, an annual event aimed at mobilising resources and raising awareness to address environmental degradation in...

item.title: New Zealand Defeats Sri Lanka by 61 Runs, Eliminating Hosts from T20 World Cup
item.description: Sri Lanka has been eliminated from the T20 World Cup after suffering a 61-run defeat to New Zealand in Colombo. Entering the match with hopes of reaching the semi-finals, Sri Lanka initially had contr...

item.title: Real Madrid defeats Benfica 2-1 to advance to Champions League Round of 16
item.description: Real Madrid secured their place in the Round of 16 of the UEFA Champions League after a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Benfica in the second leg, completing a 3-1 aggregate win. The Spanish giants showe...
